javadoc_before:
/** *

* Test for the equality of two real vectors. If all coordinates of two real * vectors are exactly the same, and none are {@code NaN}, the two real * vectors are considered to be equal. {@code NaN} coordinates are * considered to affect globally the vector and be equals to each other - * i.e, if either (or all) coordinates of the real vector are equal to * {@code NaN}, the real vector is equal to a vector with all {@code NaN} * coordinates. *

*

* This method must be overriden by concrete subclasses of * {@link RealVector}. *

* * @param other Object to test for equality. * @return {@code true} if two vector objects are equal, {@code false} if * {@code other} is null, not an instance of {@code RealVector}, or * not equal to this {@code RealVector} instance. */
javadoc_after:
/** *

* Test for the equality of two real vectors. If all coordinates of two real * vectors are exactly the same, and none are {@code NaN}, the two real * vectors are considered to be equal. {@code NaN} coordinates are * considered to affect globally the vector and be equals to each other - * i.e, if either (or all) coordinates of the real vector are equal to * {@code NaN}, the real vector is equal to a vector with all {@code NaN} * coordinates. *

*

* This method must be overriden by concrete subclasses of * {@link RealVector} (the current implementation throws an exception). *

* * @param other Object to test for equality. * @return {@code true} if two vector objects are equal, {@code false} if * {@code other} is null, not an instance of {@code RealVector}, or * not equal to this {@code RealVector} instance. * @throws MathUnsupportedOperationException if this method is not * overridden. */
